# MailKit

> MailKit configures custom domain email setup in 5 minutes for $5
> one-time. Cloudflare Email Routing for receiving + Brevo SMTP relay
> for sending + guided Gmail Send-As wizard. Indie hackers and small
> business owners get professional email on their own domain without
> paying for Google Workspace.

## Documentation
- [Landing](https://getmailkit.com/en): full product overview
- [How it works](https://getmailkit.com/en#how-it-works): 3-step automation + 1 manual Gmail step
- [Pricing](https://getmailkit.com/en#pricing): $5 one-time per mailbox setup, no subscription
- [FAQ](https://getmailkit.com/en#faq): 19 direct answers to buyer questions
- [Trust](https://getmailkit.com/en#trust): guarantee + auto-refund policy
- [Terms](https://getmailkit.com/en/terms): legal terms of service
- [Privacy](https://getmailkit.com/en/privacy): data handling policy

## Russian
- [Lending RU](https://getmailkit.com/ru)
- [FAQ RU](https://getmailkit.com/ru#faq)
- [Terms RU](https://getmailkit.com/ru/terms)
- [Privacy RU](https://getmailkit.com/ru/privacy)

## Use cases
- Indie hackers wanting hello@product.com instead of personal Gmail
- SMB owners needing info@business.com without paying $6/user/month for Google Workspace
- Freelancers and consultants wanting professional domain email tied to their personal Gmail
- Small agencies setting up email for individual clients (one-time fee per mailbox)
- Non-English entrepreneurs (Russia, EU, Latin America) where Google Workspace pricing
  is disproportionate to local SMB economics

## Key facts
- Cost: $5 USD one-time per mailbox setup
- Time: 5 minutes end to end (15s Cloudflare + 60s Brevo + 3min guided Gmail Send-As)
- DIY equivalent: 60-90 minutes for an experienced engineer, 3+ hours for everyone else
- Sending limit: 500 emails per day per domain (50/hour, 5/minute burst caps)
- Refund: automatic within 24 hours if our automation fails; 30-day functional guarantee otherwise
- Aliases: unlimited free aliases via Cloudflare Email Routing (anything@yourdomain.com)
- Required: domain DNS on Cloudflare (free tier sufficient), a personal Gmail account
- Not required: Google Workspace, Brevo account, paid Cloudflare plan

## Architecture
- Receiving: Cloudflare Email Routing (free tier, MX records, forwarding rules)
- Sending: Brevo SMTP relay under MailKit's shared corporate account, with per-domain
  authentication (DKIM, SPF, DMARC)
- Outbound origin: customer's personal Gmail via Send-As feature, configured through
  the 3-minute guided wizard
- Runtime: Next.js 16 App Router on Vercel, Supabase Postgres for state, Lemon Squeezy
  as Merchant of Record for payments

## Comparisons
- vs Google Workspace ($6/user/month, separate inbox, $72/year recurring): MailKit is
  one-time $5, uses your existing Gmail, no migration if you stop using it
- vs ImprovMX (free forwarding only): MailKit adds outbound sending, not just receiving
- vs Mailgun / Postmark / SendGrid: those are SMTP-relay APIs for developers; MailKit
  is end-to-end domain email setup for non-developers via guided wizard

- Support: support@getmailkit.com
